concept: Seal / Authentication Mark / To Stamp Official
description: A device for stamping an authenticating mark; to close or authenticate. 'Paku' names what confirms—the mark that proves authenticity or closure.
sound_symbolism: The word stamps firm. 'Pa' presses with decisive action, seal applied. Then 'ku' strikes the mark, impression made.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo paku' (the seal). As verb: 'mi paku' (I seal). For authentication, closing letters, official marks.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Seals confirmed identity—the personal mark that authenticated documents across distance.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Authenticity as practice—the seal represents integrity, being true to one's word.
